title: Lush Tangy Uruguayan Zucchini Gratin
description: A tangy, dense, adventurous Uruguayan zucchini gratin that blends smoky wood-fired heat with bright lime and fresh cilantro, served straight from a kadhai for a rustic twist.
instructions: Heat the kadhai over medium heat and pour in the olive oil, swirling to coat the bottom.
Add the sliced onion and minced garlic, sautéing until the onion turns translucent and fragrant.
Stir in the zucchini slices, cooking until they just begin to soften, about 5 minutes.
Pour in the tomato puree, smoked paprika, cumin, cayenne, and a pinch of salt and pepper, then simmer the mixture for 10 minutes, allowing the flavors to meld.
Remove the kadhai from heat, stir in the lime juice and zest, chopped cilantro, and half of the crumbled queso fresco, adjusting seasoning as needed.
Transfer the mixture to a preheated wood-fired oven set at 375°F, placing it in a shallow pan or directly in the oven if using a cast-iron skillet.
Bake for 15 minutes, then sprinkle the remaining queso fresco and a tablespoon of butter on top before returning to the oven for another 5 minutes to create a golden crust.
Remove from the oven, let rest for 5 minutes, then serve hot straight from the kadhai for an authentic Uruguayan twist.
